concerns.text: Phase 7 (T036–T040) introduces a 'Metabolic Cost Proxy' and 'quarter-power exponent' testing. This creates a semantic dependency violation: the spec.md (Assumptions) and plan.md explicitly state that explicit metabolic energy modeling is 'out of scope' and only a 'total connectivity strength' covariate is authorized. T036–T040 consume artifacts (Q values) to produce a new class of results not defined in the upstream spec, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' rule where the 'Producer' (Spec) did not authorize the 'Consumer' (Task) to generate this specific artifact type. This is unauthorized scope creep.

Task T011 outputs 'separate preprocessed behavioral CSV and fMRI time series/matrix CSV (no merged metrics yet)'. Task T013 mandates 'median imputation' for missing behavioral data. However, T025 (US3) requires merging these datasets. The dependency chain is broken: T011/T013 do not produce a unified 'Subject' artifact with imputed values ready for the merge in T025. T025 implicitly requires a transformation step (imputation + merge) that is not explicitly defined as a preceding task, creating a data-flow gap where the consumer (T025) expects an artifact state (merged, imputed) that the producer (T011/T013) does not guarantee.

Task T029 requires performing sensitivity analysis with thresholds varying in '[deferred] increments' and lists five specific '[deferred]' placeholders. This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule for executable tasks: the task description contains unresolvable parameters (placeholders) that prevent the task from being executed or tested. A task cannot be a valid 'Producer' of results if its input parameters are undefined placeholders, breaking the deterministic flow required for the subsequent analysis tasks (T030).

Task T012 specifies a band-pass filter of '–0.1 Hz' (negative frequency). This is a semantic error in the task definition that contradicts the physical reality of the data flow. While T012 is a 'Producer' of time-series data, the instruction contains a fatal logical flaw (negative frequency) that will cause the produced artifact to be invalid or the script to fail, breaking the dependency for all downstream tasks (T013, T018, etc.) that rely on valid time-series input.

Tasks T036 and T037 depend on a 'Metabolic Cost Proxy' metric. However, the upstream 'Producer' (Spec/Plan) only authorized 'total connectivity strength' as a covariate. T036 attempts to define a new metric ('Metabolic Cost Proxy') based on 'total edge weight and graph density' which is not a defined artifact in the data-model or spec. This creates a 'Consumer' (T037) that depends on a 'Producer' (T036) generating an artifact type that was not validated or defined in the foundational layers (Spec/Plan), violating the semantic layering of the plan.
